title: Redirect Revalidation, DNS, Address Classes, Proxies, and SSRF · Compiler Case: Proxy Egress Policy
summary: A URL passes string validation once, then redirects or resolves to loopback, private, link-local, metadata, mixed-address, or rebinding targets through an ambient proxy. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts proxy egress policy increment in connection-time SSRF defense record. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Make proxy inheritance, bypass rules, egress allowlists, metadata blocks, and connection evidence explicit.
Bind validated resolution to connection behavior or an egress proxy and verify re-resolution and mixed-answer cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 5798 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to block unsafe schemes, addresses, redirects, rebinding, proxies, and metadata while retaining causal evidence; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to block unsafe schemes, addresses, redirects, rebinding, proxies, and metadata while retaining causal evidence; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then change one duplicate, accessibility state, axe result, resource budget, browser readiness, or manual-review condition without overclaiming. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Make proxy inheritance, bypass rules, egress allowlists, metadata blocks, and connection evidence explicit.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Proxy Egress Policy model · Theory Proxy Egress…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Application URL validation alone controls all outbound destinations.
